name: Single-Leg Romanian Deadlift (Bodyweight)
resistance_profile: Hinging over one leg loads the hamstring under stretch at the bottom of the reach.
rom_notes: Hinge until you feel a clear hamstring stretch; a soft knee, flat back.
cues: Stand on one leg with a soft knee
Hinge at the hip, reaching your chest forward and free leg back
Stop at a clear hamstring stretch, then squeeze back up
execution_steps: Stand on one leg with a soft knee, the other foot lightly off the floor.
Hinge at the hip, reaching your chest forward and your free leg straight back.
Lower until you feel a clear hamstring stretch, keeping your hips square and back flat.
Squeeze the hamstring and glute to return to standing.
good_when: You want to train the hamstrings alongside balance and hip stability with no equipment.
You are correcting a left-to-right strength imbalance.
bad_when: Balance fails before the hamstring is challenged (use a two-leg RDL or add light support).
You want heavy bilateral loading for strength (use a barbell RDL).
common_errors: Rounding the back to reach lower
Twisting the hips open instead of keeping them square
progressions: Hold a dumbbell in one hand
regressions: Keep the back-leg toes lightly on the floor for balance
Hold a wall or chair with one hand
